\u003cstrong\u003eLedger-Organizing Graph (LOG)\u003c/strong\u003e system for building distributed AI applications from tiny, specialized agents. Unlike large monolithic models, POLLN breaks intelligence into hundreds of simple, inspectable components that coordinate through learned connections.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCore Principle\u003c/strong\u003e: Intelligence emerges from connections, not from size.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" Prompt\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTile Intelligence in real-time spreadsheets for simulation or monitoring. Deconstruct Agents into Essential functions for granular reasoning control and reverse engineering logic visually. SMPbots Seed+Model+Prompt can replace blurry logic if cell is functioning optimum and can scale. Inductive ML Programming in Spreadsheets or Embedded Headless.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePOLLN introduces \u003cstrong\u003eSMPbots\u003c/strong\u003e—a programming approach that's as stable as a CPU script but scales naturally on GPU hardware. Load the model once, run thousands of calculations in parallel on CUDA or Kubernetes, locally or in the cloud.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGPU-Stable Programming for CUDA and Kubernetes\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAt the surface level, SMP is approachable for spreadsheet users. Under the hood, it's a GPU-native programming model that scales horizontally across CUDA cores and Kubernetes clusters without code changes.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eTile Intelligence: Deconstructing Agents into Essential Functions\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-tile-intelligence-deconstructing-agents-into-essential-functions\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Tile Intelligence: Deconstructing Agents into Essential Functions\" href=\"#tile-intelligence-deconstructing-agents-into-essential-functions\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTraditional AI agents are monolithic black boxes. POLLN \u003cstrong\u003edeconstructs agents into tiles\u003c/strong\u003e—essential functions that provide:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGranular reasoning control\u003c/strong\u003e - See each step of the logic\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVisual reverse engineering\u003c/strong\u003e - Understand how decisions emerge\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eComposable intelligence\u003c/strong\u003e - Mix and match tiles like LEGO\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│           FROM MONOLITHIC TO TILE INTELLIGENCE            │\n├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│                                                             │\n│   MONOLITHIC AGENT          TILE ARCHITECTURE              │\n│                                                             │\n│   ┌─────────────────┐         ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐    │\n│   │                 │         │ Sense │ Reason│ Act │    │\n│   │  [Black Box]    │         │ Tile │  Tile │Tile │    │\n│   │                 │         └──┬──┘ └──┬──┘ └─┬──┘    │\n│   │  Input→Output   │            │     │     │ \u003cem\u003eis\u003c/em\u003e simple for the user. But that simplicity doesn't mean it's not powerful. In fact, for spreadsheet applications, SMP Programming is \u003cstrong\u003eoften better\u003c/strong\u003e than complex AI systems.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eInductive ML Programming in Spreadsheets:\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLearn patterns FROM data, not just apply rules TO data\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eEach cell induces its own logic from examples\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eEmbedded headless operation—no UI needed, just calculation\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" calculation\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e❌ Network latency (100-500ms per call)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e❌ Rate limits and costs\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e❌ No offline capability\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e❌ Can't parallelize efficiently\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSMPbots approach:\u003c/strong\u003e Run calculations in parallel with in-memory models\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e✅ Millisecond response times (model already loaded)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e✅ Thousands of parallel calculations on CUDA cores\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e✅ Scales horizontally on Kubernetes clusters\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e✅ Same code runs locally or in the cloud\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e✅ Works offline after initial load\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e✅ Perfect for batch operations\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGPU Programming, Simplified:\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSMP scripts are as stable as traditional CPU code, but they execute on GPU hardware when available. The runtime handles:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eCUDA memory management automatically\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eKubernetes pod scheduling for distributed workloads\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFallback to CPU when GPU unavailable\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eNo code changes needed for different deployment targets\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe \"Simple Person Programming\" Advantage\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-the-simple-person-programming-advantage\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: The \u0026quot;Simple Person Programming\u0026quot; Advantage\" href=\"#the-simple-person-programming-advantage\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSMP Programming democratizes AI development:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"Traditional AI Engineer:\n  \u0026quot;I need to fine-tune a transformer, set up inference,\n   deploy a microservice, handle authentication...\u0026quot;\n\nSMP Programmer (Spreadsheet User):\n  \u0026quot;I just want this cell to tell me if sales are trending up.\u0026quot;\n  =SMP(\u0026quot;trend\u0026quot;, A1:A100, \u0026quot;up or down?\u0026quot;)\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eTraditional AI Engineer:\n  \"I need to fine-tune a transformer, set up inference,\n   deploy a microservice, handle authentication...\"\n\nSMP Programmer (Spreadsheet User):\n  \"I just want this cell to tell me if sales are trending up.\"\n  =SMP(\"trend\", A1:A100, \"up or down?\")\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe complexity is \u003cstrong\u003ehandled by the system\u003c/strong\u003e, not the user. The user gets powerful AI without needing to be an AI engineer.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eKV-Cache System\u003c/strong\u003e - Shared context between cells\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHebbian Learning\u003c/strong\u003e - Cells that work well together wire together\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePlinko Selection\u003c/strong\u003e - Probabilistic choice for diversity\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWorld Model\u003c/strong\u003e - Internal representation for dreaming\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFederated Learning\u003c/strong\u003e - Knowledge sharing across cells\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCUDA-K8s Runtime\u003c/strong\u003e - Automatic GPU scaling and cluster orchestration\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe simplicity is in the \u003cstrong\u003einterface\u003c/strong\u003e, not the \u003cstrong\u003eimplementation\u003c/strong\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGPU Programming That Just Works:\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSMP scripts are portable across execution environments:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWrite once on your laptop (CPU or GPU)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDeploy to Kubernetes cluster (distributed GPU)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eScale horizontally without code changes\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eRuntime handles the complexity\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSMPbot class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: 3-round production development sprint - 12 major systems

## Round 1: Foundation (4 agents)

### 1. Sprint 8 Security Implementation
- JWT authentication with access/refresh tokens
- Rate limiting (colony/user/resource levels)
- Enhanced input validation (XSS, prototype pollution)
- A2A package signing (HMAC-SHA512)
- Federated sync encryption (AES-256-GCM)
- Security configuration system
- Audit logging (13 event types)
- CORS & CSP support
- 59 security tests passing

### 2. Performance Benchmarking Suite
- 70 benchmarks across 7 suites
- Statistical analysis (mean, median, p95, p99)
- Memory profiling integration
- HTML reports with Chart.js
- CLI: npm run bench, npm run bench:compare

### 3. Examples Expansion (4 new demos)
- workflow-orchestration/ - 7-stage document pipeline
- federated-colony/ - Privacy-preserving FL
- safety-first/ - 23 constitutional constraints
- streaming-agent/ - Real-time WebSocket streaming

### 4. API Documentation Generator
- Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI spec
- TypeScript client SDK (src/api/client/)
- Interactive documentation (Redoc)
- CLI: npm run docs:api, npm run docs:api:serve

## Round 2: Advanced Features (4 agents)

### 5. LoRA Library Prototype
- LoRA tool belt system (src/core/lora/)
- 4 expert LoRAs (code, analyst, writer, researcher)
- Distillation pipeline
- CLI: npm run lora:*, 6 commands

### 6. Emergent Intelligence Detection
- Hydraulic framework (pressure/flow/valves/pumps/reservoirs)
- Emergence detection and cataloging
- Enhanced stigmergy with decay/visualization
- Web dashboard for real-time monitoring
- CLI: npm run emergence:*, npm run hydraulic:*

### 7. Advanced Federation Protocols
- 4 aggregation strategies (FedAvg, FedProx, Async, Adaptive)
- Privacy enhancements (DP, secure aggregation)
- Fault tolerance (Byzantine resilience, checkpoints)
- Network protocols (P2P, gossip)
- 28 tests (21 passing)

### 8. Multi-Colony Orchestration
- ColonyManager (orchestrator, scheduler, load-balancer)
- Inter-colony communication (bridge, protocol, queue)
- Colony lifecycle (provision, decommission, migrate, scale)
- CLI: npm run colony:*, 9 commands

## Round 3: Production Readiness (4 agents)

### 9. Deployment Automation
- Docker containerization (Dockerfile, docker-compose)
- Kubernetes manifests (deployment, service, ingress, HPA, PDB)
- Terraform infrastructure (AWS EKS, RDS, S3, Route53)
- Deployment scripts (deploy.sh, rollback.sh, health-check.sh)

### 10. Monitoring & Observability
- OpenTelemetry metrics (50+ metrics)
- Distributed tracing (Jaeger, Zipkin, OTLP)
- Structured logging (Pino)
- Health checks (liveness/readiness/startup)
- Alerting (threshold-based, multi-channel)
- Grafana dashboard
- CLI: npm run monitor:*

### 11. Scaling Strategies
- Auto-scaling manager (reactive, proactive, predictive, cost-optimized)
- Resource management (allocator, scheduler, balancer, throttler)
- 9 pre-configured scaling policies
- Predictive scaling with ML (15-min horizon)
- CLI: npm run scale:*, 5 commands
- 49 tests passing

### 12. Disaster Recovery
- Backup system (full, incremental, snapshot)
- Storage backends (local, S3, GCS, Azure)
- Restore system (validation, migration, consistency)
- Failover system (hot/cold/multi-active strategies)
- RTO < 15min, RPO < 5min
- CLI: npm run backup:*, npm run failover:*

## Statistics
- 247 files changed
- 30,000+ lines of production code
- 200+ tests added
- 50+ new CLI commands
- 12 major systems delivered

class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: 3-round production development sprint - 12 major systems

## Round 1: Foundation (4 agents)

### 1. Sprint 8 Security Implementation
- JWT authentication with access/refresh tokens
- Rate limiting (colony/user/resource levels)
- Enhanced input validation (XSS, prototype pollution)
- A2A package signing (HMAC-SHA512)
- Federated sync encryption (AES-256-GCM)
- Security configuration system
- Audit logging (13 event types)
- CORS & CSP support
- 59 security tests passing

### 2. Performance Benchmarking Suite
- 70 benchmarks across 7 suites
- Statistical analysis (mean, median, p95, p99)
- Memory profiling integration
- HTML reports with Chart.js
- CLI: npm run bench, npm run bench:compare

### 3. Examples Expansion (4 new demos)
- workflow-orchestration/ - 7-stage document pipeline
- federated-colony/ - Privacy-preserving FL
- safety-first/ - 23 constitutional constraints
- streaming-agent/ - Real-time WebSocket streaming

### 4. API Documentation Generator
- Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI spec
- TypeScript client SDK (src/api/client/)
- Interactive documentation (Redoc)
- CLI: npm run docs:api, npm run docs:api:serve

## Round 2: Advanced Features (4 agents)

### 5. LoRA Library Prototype
- LoRA tool belt system (src/core/lora/)
- 4 expert LoRAs (code, analyst, writer, researcher)
- Distillation pipeline
- CLI: npm run lora:*, 6 commands

### 6. Emergent Intelligence Detection
- Hydraulic framework (pressure/flow/valves/pumps/reservoirs)
- Emergence detection and cataloging
- Enhanced stigmergy with decay/visualization
- Web dashboard for real-time monitoring
- CLI: npm run emergence:*, npm run hydraulic:*

### 7. Advanced Federation Protocols
- 4 aggregation strategies (FedAvg, FedProx, Async, Adaptive)
- Privacy enhancements (DP, secure aggregation)
- Fault tolerance (Byzantine resilience, checkpoints)
- Network protocols (P2P, gossip)
- 28 tests (21 passing)

### 8. Multi-Colony Orchestration
- ColonyManager (orchestrator, scheduler, load-balancer)
- Inter-colony communication (bridge, protocol, queue)
- Colony lifecycle (provision, decommission, migrate, scale)
- CLI: npm run colony:*, 9 commands

## Round 3: Production Readiness (4 agents)

### 9. Deployment Automation
- Docker containerization (Dockerfile, docker-compose)
- Kubernetes manifests (deployment, service, ingress, HPA, PDB)
- Terraform infrastructure (AWS EKS, RDS, S3, Route53)
- Deployment scripts (deploy.sh, rollback.sh, health-check.sh)

### 10. Monitoring & Observability
- OpenTelemetry metrics (50+ metrics)
- Distributed tracing (Jaeger, Zipkin, OTLP)
- Structured logging (Pino)
- Health checks (liveness/readiness/startup)
- Alerting (threshold-based, multi-channel)
- Grafana dashboard
- CLI: npm run monitor:*

### 11. Scaling Strategies
- Auto-scaling manager (reactive, proactive, predictive, cost-optimized)
- Resource management (allocator, scheduler, balancer, throttler)
- 9 pre-configured scaling policies
- Predictive scaling with ML (15-min horizon)
- CLI: npm run scale:*, 5 commands
- 49 tests passing

### 12. Disaster Recovery
- Backup system (full, incremental, snapshot)
- Storage backends (local, S3, GCS, Azure)
- Restore system (validation, migration, consistency)
- Failover system (hot/cold/multi-active strategies)
- RTO < 15min, RPO < 5min
- CLI: npm run backup:*, npm run failover:*

## Statistics
- 247 files changed
- 30,000+ lines of production code
- 200+ tests added
- 50+ new CLI commands
- 12 major systems delivered

class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: 3-round production development sprint - 12 major systems

## Round 1: Foundation (4 agents)

### 1. Sprint 8 Security Implementation
- JWT authentication with access/refresh tokens
- Rate limiting (colony/user/resource levels)
- Enhanced input validation (XSS, prototype pollution)
- A2A package signing (HMAC-SHA512)
- Federated sync encryption (AES-256-GCM)
- Security configuration system
- Audit logging (13 event types)
- CORS & CSP support
- 59 security tests passing

### 2. Performance Benchmarking Suite
- 70 benchmarks across 7 suites
- Statistical analysis (mean, median, p95, p99)
- Memory profiling integration
- HTML reports with Chart.js
- CLI: npm run bench, npm run bench:compare

### 3. Examples Expansion (4 new demos)
- workflow-orchestration/ - 7-stage document pipeline
- federated-colony/ - Privacy-preserving FL
- safety-first/ - 23 constitutional constraints
- streaming-agent/ - Real-time WebSocket streaming

### 4. API Documentation Generator
- Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI spec
- TypeScript client SDK (src/api/client/)
- Interactive documentation (Redoc)
- CLI: npm run docs:api, npm run docs:api:serve

## Round 2: Advanced Features (4 agents)

### 5. LoRA Library Prototype
- LoRA tool belt system (src/core/lora/)
- 4 expert LoRAs (code, analyst, writer, researcher)
- Distillation pipeline
- CLI: npm run lora:*, 6 commands

### 6. Emergent Intelligence Detection
- Hydraulic framework (pressure/flow/valves/pumps/reservoirs)
- Emergence detection and cataloging
- Enhanced stigmergy with decay/visualization
- Web dashboard for real-time monitoring
- CLI: npm run emergence:*, npm run hydraulic:*

### 7. Advanced Federation Protocols
- 4 aggregation strategies (FedAvg, FedProx, Async, Adaptive)
- Privacy enhancements (DP, secure aggregation)
- Fault tolerance (Byzantine resilience, checkpoints)
- Network protocols (P2P, gossip)
- 28 tests (21 passing)

### 8. Multi-Colony Orchestration
- ColonyManager (orchestrator, scheduler, load-balancer)
- Inter-colony communication (bridge, protocol, queue)
- Colony lifecycle (provision, decommission, migrate, scale)
- CLI: npm run colony:*, 9 commands

## Round 3: Production Readiness (4 agents)

### 9. Deployment Automation
- Docker containerization (Dockerfile, docker-compose)
- Kubernetes manifests (deployment, service, ingress, HPA, PDB)
- Terraform infrastructure (AWS EKS, RDS, S3, Route53)
- Deployment scripts (deploy.sh, rollback.sh, health-check.sh)

### 10. Monitoring & Observability
- OpenTelemetry metrics (50+ metrics)
- Distributed tracing (Jaeger, Zipkin, OTLP)
- Structured logging (Pino)
- Health checks (liveness/readiness/startup)
- Alerting (threshold-based, multi-channel)
- Grafana dashboard
- CLI: npm run monitor:*

### 11. Scaling Strategies
- Auto-scaling manager (reactive, proactive, predictive, cost-optimized)
- Resource management (allocator, scheduler, balancer, throttler)
- 9 pre-configured scaling policies
- Predictive scaling with ML (15-min horizon)
- CLI: npm run scale:*, 5 commands
- 49 tests passing

### 12. Disaster Recovery
- Backup system (full, incremental, snapshot)
- Storage backends (local, S3, GCS, Azure)
- Restore system (validation, migration, consistency)
- Failover system (hot/cold/multi-active strategies)
- RTO < 15min, RPO < 5min
- CLI: npm run backup:*, npm run failover:*

## Statistics
- 247 files changed
- 30,000+ lines of production code
- 200+ tests added
- 50+ new CLI commands
- 12 major systems delivered

class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: 3-round production development sprint - 12 major systems

## Round 1: Foundation (4 agents)

### 1. Sprint 8 Security Implementation
- JWT authentication with access/refresh tokens
- Rate limiting (colony/user/resource levels)
- Enhanced input validation (XSS, prototype pollution)
- A2A package signing (HMAC-SHA512)
- Federated sync encryption (AES-256-GCM)
- Security configuration system
- Audit logging (13 event types)
- CORS & CSP support
- 59 security tests passing

### 2. Performance Benchmarking Suite
- 70 benchmarks across 7 suites
- Statistical analysis (mean, median, p95, p99)
- Memory profiling integration
- HTML reports with Chart.js
- CLI: npm run bench, npm run bench:compare

### 3. Examples Expansion (4 new demos)
- workflow-orchestration/ - 7-stage document pipeline
- federated-colony/ - Privacy-preserving FL
- safety-first/ - 23 constitutional constraints
- streaming-agent/ - Real-time WebSocket streaming

### 4. API Documentation Generator
- Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI spec
- TypeScript client SDK (src/api/client/)
- Interactive documentation (Redoc)
- CLI: npm run docs:api, npm run docs:api:serve

## Round 2: Advanced Features (4 agents)

### 5. LoRA Library Prototype
- LoRA tool belt system (src/core/lora/)
- 4 expert LoRAs (code, analyst, writer, researcher)
- Distillation pipeline
- CLI: npm run lora:*, 6 commands

### 6. Emergent Intelligence Detection
- Hydraulic framework (pressure/flow/valves/pumps/reservoirs)
- Emergence detection and cataloging
- Enhanced stigmergy with decay/visualization
- Web dashboard for real-time monitoring
- CLI: npm run emergence:*, npm run hydraulic:*

### 7. Advanced Federation Protocols
- 4 aggregation strategies (FedAvg, FedProx, Async, Adaptive)
- Privacy enhancements (DP, secure aggregation)
- Fault tolerance (Byzantine resilience, checkpoints)
- Network protocols (P2P, gossip)
- 28 tests (21 passing)

### 8. Multi-Colony Orchestration
- ColonyManager (orchestrator, scheduler, load-balancer)
- Inter-colony communication (bridge, protocol, queue)
- Colony lifecycle (provision, decommission, migrate, scale)
- CLI: npm run colony:*, 9 commands

## Round 3: Production Readiness (4 agents)

### 9. Deployment Automation
- Docker containerization (Dockerfile, docker-compose)
- Kubernetes manifests (deployment, service, ingress, HPA, PDB)
- Terraform infrastructure (AWS EKS, RDS, S3, Route53)
- Deployment scripts (deploy.sh, rollback.sh, health-check.sh)

### 10. Monitoring & Observability
- OpenTelemetry metrics (50+ metrics)
- Distributed tracing (Jaeger, Zipkin, OTLP)
- Structured logging (Pino)
- Health checks (liveness/readiness/startup)
- Alerting (threshold-based, multi-channel)
- Grafana dashboard
- CLI: npm run monitor:*

### 11. Scaling Strategies
- Auto-scaling manager (reactive, proactive, predictive, cost-optimized)
- Resource management (allocator, scheduler, balancer, throttler)
- 9 pre-configured scaling policies
- Predictive scaling with ML (15-min horizon)
- CLI: npm run scale:*, 5 commands
- 49 tests passing

### 12. Disaster Recovery
- Backup system (full, incremental, snapshot)
- Storage backends (local, S3, GCS, Azure)
- Restore system (validation, migration, consistency)
- Failover system (hot/cold/multi-active strategies)
- RTO < 15min, RPO < 5min
- CLI: npm run backup:*, npm run failover:*

## Statistics
- 247 files changed
- 30,000+ lines of production code
- 200+ tests added
- 50+ new CLI commands
- 12 major systems delivered

### 2. Performance Benchmarking Suite
- 70 benchmarks across 7 suites
- Statistical analysis (mean, median, p95, p99)
- Memory profiling integration
- HTML reports with Chart.js
- CLI: npm run bench, npm run bench:compare

### 3. Examples Expansion (4 new demos)
- workflow-orchestration/ - 7-stage document pipeline
- federated-colony/ - Privacy-preserving FL
- safety-first/ - 23 constitutional constraints
- streaming-agent/ - Real-time WebSocket streaming

### 4. API Documentation Generator
- Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI spec
- TypeScript client SDK (src/api/client/)
- Interactive documentation (Redoc)
- CLI: npm run docs:api, npm run docs:api:serve

## Round 2: Advanced Features (4 agents)

### 5. LoRA Library Prototype
- LoRA tool belt system (src/core/lora/)
- 4 expert LoRAs (code, analyst, writer, researcher)
- Distillation pipeline
- CLI: npm run lora:*, 6 commands

### 6. Emergent Intelligence Detection
- Hydraulic framework (pressure/flow/valves/pumps/reservoirs)
- Emergence detection and cataloging
- Enhanced stigmergy with decay/visualization
- Web dashboard for real-time monitoring
- CLI: npm run emergence:*, npm run hydraulic:*

### 7. Advanced Federation Protocols
- 4 aggregation strategies (FedAvg, FedProx, Async, Adaptive)
- Privacy enhancements (DP, secure aggregation)
- Fault tolerance (Byzantine resilience, checkpoints)
- Network protocols (P2P, gossip)
- 28 tests (21 passing)

### 8. Multi-Colony Orchestration
- ColonyManager (orchestrator, scheduler, load-balancer)
- Inter-colony communication (bridge, protocol, queue)
- Colony lifecycle (provision, decommission, migrate, scale)
- CLI: npm run colony:*, 9 commands

## Round 3: Production Readiness (4 agents)

### 9. Deployment Automation
- Docker containerization (Dockerfile, docker-compose)
- Kubernetes manifests (deployment, service, ingress, HPA, PDB)
- Terraform infrastructure (AWS EKS, RDS, S3, Route53)
- Deployment scripts (deploy.sh, rollback.sh, health-check.sh)

### 10. Monitoring & Observability
- OpenTelemetry metrics (50+ metrics)
- Distributed tracing (Jaeger, Zipkin, OTLP)
- Structured logging (Pino)
- Health checks (liveness/readiness/startup)
- Alerting (threshold-based, multi-channel)
- Grafana dashboard
- CLI: npm run monitor:*

### 11. Scaling Strategies
- Auto-scaling manager (reactive, proactive, predictive, cost-optimized)
- Resource management (allocator, scheduler, balancer, throttler)
- 9 pre-configured scaling policies
- Predictive scaling with ML (15-min horizon)
- CLI: npm run scale:*, 5 commands
- 49 tests passing

### 12. Disaster Recovery
- Backup system (full, incremental, snapshot)
- Storage backends (local, S3, GCS, Azure)
- Restore system (validation, migration, consistency)
- Failover system (hot/cold/multi-active strategies)
- RTO < 15min, RPO < 5min
- CLI: npm run backup:*, npm run failover:*

- 247 files changed
- 30,000+ lines of production code
- 200+ tests added
- 50+ new CLI commands
- 12 major systems delivered

class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: 3-round production development sprint - 12 major systems

## Round 1: Foundation (4 agents)

### 1. Sprint 8 Security Implementation
- JWT authentication with access/refresh tokens
- Rate limiting (colony/user/resource levels)
- Enhanced input validation (XSS, prototype pollution)
- A2A package signing (HMAC-SHA512)
- Federated sync encryption (AES-256-GCM)
- Security configuration system
- Audit logging (13 event types)
- CORS & CSP support
- 59 security tests passing

### 2. Performance Benchmarking Suite
- 70 benchmarks across 7 suites
- Statistical analysis (mean, median, p95, p99)
- Memory profiling integration
- HTML reports with Chart.js
- CLI: npm run bench, npm run bench:compare

### 3. Examples Expansion (4 new demos)
- workflow-orchestration/ - 7-stage document pipeline
- federated-colony/ - Privacy-preserving FL
- safety-first/ - 23 constitutional constraints
- streaming-agent/ - Real-time WebSocket streaming

### 4. API Documentation Generator
- Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI spec
- TypeScript client SDK (src/api/client/)
- Interactive documentation (Redoc)
- CLI: npm run docs:api, npm run docs:api:serve

## Round 2: Advanced Features (4 agents)

### 5. LoRA Library Prototype
- LoRA tool belt system (src/core/lora/)
- 4 expert LoRAs (code, analyst, writer, researcher)
- Distillation pipeline
- CLI: npm run lora:*, 6 commands

### 6. Emergent Intelligence Detection
- Hydraulic framework (pressure/flow/valves/pumps/reservoirs)
- Emergence detection and cataloging
- Enhanced stigmergy with decay/visualization
- Web dashboard for real-time monitoring
- CLI: npm run emergence:*, npm run hydraulic:*

### 7. Advanced Federation Protocols
- 4 aggregation strategies (FedAvg, FedProx, Async, Adaptive)
- Privacy enhancements (DP, secure aggregation)
- Fault tolerance (Byzantine resilience, checkpoints)
- Network protocols (P2P, gossip)
- 28 tests (21 passing)

### 8. Multi-Colony Orchestration
- ColonyManager (orchestrator, scheduler, load-balancer)
- Inter-colony communication (bridge, protocol, queue)
- Colony lifecycle (provision, decommission, migrate, scale)
- CLI: npm run colony:*, 9 commands

## Round 3: Production Readiness (4 agents)

### 9. Deployment Automation
- Docker containerization (Dockerfile, docker-compose)
- Kubernetes manifests (deployment, service, ingress, HPA, PDB)
- Terraform infrastructure (AWS EKS, RDS, S3, Route53)
- Deployment scripts (deploy.sh, rollback.sh, health-check.sh)

### 10. Monitoring & Observability
- OpenTelemetry metrics (50+ metrics)
- Distributed tracing (Jaeger, Zipkin, OTLP)
- Structured logging (Pino)
- Health checks (liveness/readiness/startup)
- Alerting (threshold-based, multi-channel)
- Grafana dashboard
- CLI: npm run monitor:*

### 11. Scaling Strategies
- Auto-scaling manager (reactive, proactive, predictive, cost-optimized)
- Resource management (allocator, scheduler, balancer, throttler)
- 9 pre-configured scaling policies
- Predictive scaling with ML (15-min horizon)
- CLI: npm run scale:*, 5 commands
- 49 tests passing

### 12. Disaster Recovery
- Backup system (full, incremental, snapshot)
- Storage backends (local, S3, GCS, Azure)
- Restore system (validation, migration, consistency)
- Failover system (hot/cold/multi-active strategies)
- RTO < 15min, RPO < 5min
- CLI: npm run backup:*, npm run failover:*

## Statistics
- 247 files changed
- 30,000+ lines of production code
- 200+ tests added
- 50+ new CLI commands
- 12 major systems delivered

production development sprint - 12 major systems

## Round 1: Foundation (4 agents)

### 1. Sprint 8 Security Implementation
- JWT authentication with access/refresh tokens
- Rate limiting (colony/user/resource levels)
- Enhanced input validation (XSS, prototype pollution)
- A2A package signing (HMAC-SHA512)
- Federated sync encryption (AES-256-GCM)
- Security configuration system
- Audit logging (13 event types)
- CORS & CSP support
- 59 security tests passing

### 2. Performance Benchmarking Suite
- 70 benchmarks across 7 suites
- Statistical analysis (mean, median, p95, p99)
- Memory profiling integration
- HTML reports with Chart.js
- CLI: npm run bench, npm run bench:compare

### 3. Examples Expansion (4 new demos)
- workflow-orchestration/ - 7-stage document pipeline
- federated-colony/ - Privacy-preserving FL
- safety-first/ - 23 constitutional constraints
- streaming-agent/ - Real-time WebSocket streaming

### 4. API Documentation Generator
- Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI spec
- TypeScript client SDK (src/api/client/)
- Interactive documentation (Redoc)
- CLI: npm run docs:api, npm run docs:api:serve

## Round 2: Advanced Features (4 agents)

### 5. LoRA Library Prototype
- LoRA tool belt system (src/core/lora/)
- 4 expert LoRAs (code, analyst, writer, researcher)
- Distillation pipeline
- CLI: npm run lora:*, 6 commands

### 6. Emergent Intelligence Detection
- Hydraulic framework (pressure/flow/valves/pumps/reservoirs)
- Emergence detection and cataloging
- Enhanced stigmergy with decay/visualization
- Web dashboard for real-time monitoring
- CLI: npm run emergence:*, npm run hydraulic:*

### 7. Advanced Federation Protocols
- 4 aggregation strategies (FedAvg, FedProx, Async, Adaptive)
- Privacy enhancements (DP, secure aggregation)
- Fault tolerance (Byzantine resilience, checkpoints)
- Network protocols (P2P, gossip)
- 28 tests (21 passing)

### 8. Multi-Colony Orchestration
- ColonyManager (orchestrator, scheduler, load-balancer)
- Inter-colony communication (bridge, protocol, queue)
- Colony lifecycle (provision, decommission, migrate, scale)
- CLI: npm run colony:*, 9 commands

## Round 3: Production Readiness (4 agents)

### 9. Deployment Automation
- Docker containerization (Dockerfile, docker-compose)
- Kubernetes manifests (deployment, service, ingress, HPA, PDB)
- Terraform infrastructure (AWS EKS, RDS, S3, Route53)
- Deployment scripts (deploy.sh, rollback.sh, health-check.sh)

### 10. Monitoring & Observability
- OpenTelemetry metrics (50+ metrics)
- Distributed tracing (Jaeger, Zipkin, OTLP)
- Structured logging (Pino)
- Health checks (liveness/readiness/startup)
- Alerting (threshold-based, multi-channel)
- Grafana dashboard
- CLI: npm run monitor:*

### 11. Scaling Strategies
- Auto-scaling manager (reactive, proactive, predictive, cost-optimized)
- Resource management (allocator, scheduler, balancer, throttler)
- 9 pre-configured scaling policies
- Predictive scaling with ML (15-min horizon)
- CLI: npm run scale:*, 5 commands
- 49 tests passing

### 12. Disaster Recovery
- Backup system (full, incremental, snapshot)
- Storage backends (local, S3, GCS, Azure)
- Restore system (validation, migration, consistency)
- Failover system (hot/cold/multi-active strategies)
- RTO < 15min, RPO < 5min
- CLI: npm run backup:*, npm run failover:*

## Statistics
- 247 files changed
- 30,000+ lines of production code
- 200+ tests added
- 50+ new CLI commands
- 12 major systems delivered

3-round production development sprint - 12 major systems

## Round 1: Foundation (4 agents)

### 1. Sprint 8 Security Implementation
- JWT authentication with access/refresh tokens
- Rate limiting (colony/user/resource levels)
- Enhanced input validation (XSS, prototype pollution)
- A2A package signing (HMAC-SHA512)
- Federated sync encryption (AES-256-GCM)
- Security configuration system
- Audit logging (13 event types)
- CORS & CSP support
- 59 security tests passing

### 2. Performance Benchmarking Suite
- 70 benchmarks across 7 suites
- Statistical analysis (mean, median, p95, p99)
- Memory profiling integration
- HTML reports with Chart.js
- CLI: npm run bench, npm run bench:compare

### 3. Examples Expansion (4 new demos)
- workflow-orchestration/ - 7-stage document pipeline
- federated-colony/ - Privacy-preserving FL
- safety-first/ - 23 constitutional constraints
- streaming-agent/ - Real-time WebSocket streaming

### 4. API Documentation Generator
- Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI spec
- TypeScript client SDK (src/api/client/)
- Interactive documentation (Redoc)
- CLI: npm run docs:api, npm run docs:api:serve

## Round 2: Advanced Features (4 agents)

### 5. LoRA Library Prototype
- LoRA tool belt system (src/core/lora/)
- 4 expert LoRAs (code, analyst, writer, researcher)
- Distillation pipeline
- CLI: npm run lora:*, 6 commands

### 6. Emergent Intelligence Detection
- Hydraulic framework (pressure/flow/valves/pumps/reservoirs)
- Emergence detection and cataloging
- Enhanced stigmergy with decay/visualization
- Web dashboard for real-time monitoring
- CLI: npm run emergence:*, npm run hydraulic:*

### 7. Advanced Federation Protocols
- 4 aggregation strategies (FedAvg, FedProx, Async, Adaptive)
- Privacy enhancements (DP, secure aggregation)
- Fault tolerance (Byzantine resilience, checkpoints)
- Network protocols (P2P, gossip)
- 28 tests (21 passing)

### 8. Multi-Colony Orchestration
- ColonyManager (orchestrator, scheduler, load-balancer)
- Inter-colony communication (bridge, protocol, queue)
- Colony lifecycle (provision, decommission, migrate, scale)
- CLI: npm run colony:*, 9 commands

## Round 3: Production Readiness (4 agents)

### 9. Deployment Automation
- Docker containerization (Dockerfile, docker-compose)
- Kubernetes manifests (deployment, service, ingress, HPA, PDB)
- Terraform infrastructure (AWS EKS, RDS, S3, Route53)
- Deployment scripts (deploy.sh, rollback.sh, health-check.sh)

### 10. Monitoring & Observability
- OpenTelemetry metrics (50+ metrics)
- Distributed tracing (Jaeger, Zipkin, OTLP)
- Structured logging (Pino)
- Health checks (liveness/readiness/startup)
- Alerting (threshold-based, multi-channel)
- Grafana dashboard
- CLI: npm run monitor:*

### 11. Scaling Strategies
- Auto-scaling manager (reactive, proactive, predictive, cost-optimized)
- Resource management (allocator, scheduler, balancer, throttler)
- 9 pre-configured scaling policies
- Predictive scaling with ML (15-min horizon)
- CLI: npm run scale:*, 5 commands
- 49 tests passing

### 12. Disaster Recovery
- Backup system (full, incremental, snapshot)
- Storage backends (local, S3, GCS, Azure)
- Restore system (validation, migration, consistency)
- Failover system (hot/cold/multi-active strategies)
- RTO < 15min, RPO < 5min
- CLI: npm run backup:*, npm run failover:*

## Statistics
- 247 files changed
- 30,000+ lines of production code
- 200+ tests added
- 50+ new CLI commands
- 12 major systems delivered

Security & Performance Modules:
- Distributed token revocation list (revocation.ts)
- Key rotation mechanism with RSA/HMAC support (key-rotation.ts)
- A2A package cryptographic signing (a2a-signing.ts)
- Distributed rate limiting with token bucket/sliding window (rate-limit.ts)
- Memory exhaustion protection with bounded collections (memory-protection.ts)
- 246 tests passing across all new security modules

Spreadsheet Integration Research:
- UX/Product Design: User personas, journeys, UI mockups
- System Architecture: Platform integration, data models, APIs
- Knowledge Distillation: LLM-to-agent training protocols
- Technical Feasibility: Platform constraints, performance budgets
- Business Strategy: $240M ARR opportunity analysis

title="feat: Complete Waves 4-7 implementation with comprehensive research

This massive update includes:

Waves 4-7 Implementation:
- Wave 4: UI Components (CellRenderer, CellInspector, GridDisplay)
- Wave 5: Integration Layer (WebSocket, CellAPI, StateSynchronizer)
- Wave 6: Backend Infrastructure (TieredCache, CellRouter, BackendServer)
- Wave 7: Authentication (JWT, RBAC, Rate Limiting)

New Features:
- Cell Garden: Ecosystem visualization with force-directed graphs
- Cell Theater: Consciousness replay with timeline scrubbing
- Pattern Recognition: ML-based detection with 13+ pattern types
- Natural Language: Query parser with voice commands
- Performance: Virtual scrolling, object pooling, batch scheduling
- Collaboration: Yjs CRDTs, presence, conflict resolution
- GPU Acceleration: WebGPU compute shaders for batch processing
- Mobile PWA: Responsive design, service workers, offline support
- TensorFlow.js: LSTM trends, autoencoder anomalies, K-means clustering
- Redis Queues: Distributed architecture with pub/sub and streams
- Export/Import: JSON, CSV, Excel, PDF support

Research Documents (30+):
- MASTER_ROADMAP.md: 6-month implementation plan
- SECURITY_ARCHITECTURE.md: Comprehensive security guide
- MONITORING_OBSERVABILITY.md: Complete monitoring strategy
- API_GATEWAY_MESH.md: API gateway and service mesh research
- DISTRIBUTED_ARCHITECTURE.md: Scaling to millions of cells
- COLLABORATION_RESEARCH.md: Real-time collaboration
- GPU_ACCELERATION.md: GPU compute research
- MOBILE_PWA_RESEARCH.md: Mobile and PWA features
- ML_INTEGRATION_RESEARCH.md: AI/ML integration opportunities
- ANALYTICS_PIPELINE.md: Data warehouse and analytics

Infrastructure:
- Docker multi-stage builds
- Kubernetes manifests (base + production overlays)
- Terraform IaC for AWS infrastructure
- CI/CD workflows (GitHub Actions)
- Prometheus + Grafana monitoring
- Redis distributed setup
- Service worker for PWA

Stats:
- 50,000+ lines of production code
- 15,000+ lines of test code
- 30+ research documents
- 100+ new features implemented
- 821+ tests passing

title="feat: 3-round production development sprint - 12 major systems

## Round 1: Foundation (4 agents)

### 1. Sprint 8 Security Implementation
- JWT authentication with access/refresh tokens
- Rate limiting (colony/user/resource levels)
- Enhanced input validation (XSS, prototype pollution)
- A2A package signing (HMAC-SHA512)
- Federated sync encryption (AES-256-GCM)
- Security configuration system
- Audit logging (13 event types)
- CORS & CSP support
- 59 security tests passing

### 2. Performance Benchmarking Suite
- 70 benchmarks across 7 suites
- Statistical analysis (mean, median, p95, p99)
- Memory profiling integration
- HTML reports with Chart.js
- CLI: npm run bench, npm run bench:compare

### 3. Examples Expansion (4 new demos)
- workflow-orchestration/ - 7-stage document pipeline
- federated-colony/ - Privacy-preserving FL
- safety-first/ - 23 constitutional constraints
- streaming-agent/ - Real-time WebSocket streaming

### 4. API Documentation Generator
- Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI spec
- TypeScript client SDK (src/api/client/)
- Interactive documentation (Redoc)
- CLI: npm run docs:api, npm run docs:api:serve

## Round 2: Advanced Features (4 agents)

### 5. LoRA Library Prototype
- LoRA tool belt system (src/core/lora/)
- 4 expert LoRAs (code, analyst, writer, researcher)
- Distillation pipeline
- CLI: npm run lora:*, 6 commands

### 6. Emergent Intelligence Detection
- Hydraulic framework (pressure/flow/valves/pumps/reservoirs)
- Emergence detection and cataloging
- Enhanced stigmergy with decay/visualization
- Web dashboard for real-time monitoring
- CLI: npm run emergence:*, npm run hydraulic:*

### 7. Advanced Federation Protocols
- 4 aggregation strategies (FedAvg, FedProx, Async, Adaptive)
- Privacy enhancements (DP, secure aggregation)
- Fault tolerance (Byzantine resilience, checkpoints)
- Network protocols (P2P, gossip)
- 28 tests (21 passing)

### 8. Multi-Colony Orchestration
- ColonyManager (orchestrator, scheduler, load-balancer)
- Inter-colony communication (bridge, protocol, queue)
- Colony lifecycle (provision, decommission, migrate, scale)
- CLI: npm run colony:*, 9 commands

## Round 3: Production Readiness (4 agents)

### 9. Deployment Automation

This massive update includes:

Waves 4-7 Implementation:
- Wave 4: UI Components (CellRenderer, CellInspector, GridDisplay)
- Wave 5: Integration Layer (WebSocket, CellAPI, StateSynchronizer)
- Wave 6: Backend Infrastructure (TieredCache, CellRouter, BackendServer)
- Wave 7: Authentication (JWT, RBAC, Rate Limiting)

New Features:
- Cell Garden: Ecosystem visualization with force-directed graphs
- Cell Theater: Consciousness replay with timeline scrubbing
- Pattern Recognition: ML-based detection with 13+ pattern types
- Natural Language: Query parser with voice commands
- Performance: Virtual scrolling, object pooling, batch scheduling
- Collaboration: Yjs CRDTs, presence, conflict resolution
- GPU Acceleration: WebGPU compute shaders for batch processing
- Mobile PWA: Responsive design, service workers, offline support
- TensorFlow.js: LSTM trends, autoencoder anomalies, K-means clustering
- Redis Queues: Distributed architecture with pub/sub and streams
- Export/Import: JSON, CSV, Excel, PDF support

Research Documents (30+):
- MASTER_ROADMAP.md: 6-month implementation plan
- SECURITY_ARCHITECTURE.md: Comprehensive security guide
- MONITORING_OBSERVABILITY.md: Complete monitoring strategy
- API_GATEWAY_MESH.md: API gateway and service mesh research
- DISTRIBUTED_ARCHITECTURE.md: Scaling to millions of cells
- COLLABORATION_RESEARCH.md: Real-time collaboration
- GPU_ACCELERATION.md: GPU compute research
- MOBILE_PWA_RESEARCH.md: Mobile and PWA features
- ML_INTEGRATION_RESEARCH.md: AI/ML integration opportunities
- ANALYTICS_PIPELINE.md: Data warehouse and analytics

Infrastructure:
- Docker multi-stage builds
- Kubernetes manifests (base + production overlays)
- Terraform IaC for AWS infrastructure
- CI/CD workflows (GitHub Actions)
- Prometheus + Grafana monitoring
- Redis distributed setup
- Service worker for PWA

Stats:
- 50,000+ lines of production code
- 15,000+ lines of test code
- 30+ research documents
- 100+ new features implemented
- 821+ tests passing

<p dir="auto"><strong>Pattern-Organized Large Language Network - Distributed Intelligence through Inspectable Agents</strong></p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What is POLLN?</h2><a id="user-content-what-is-polln" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What is POLLN?" href="#what-is-polln"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">POLLN is a <strong>Ledger-Organizing Graph (LOG)</strong> system for building distributed AI applications from tiny, specialized agents. Unlike large monolithic models, POLLN breaks intelligence into hundreds of simple, inspectable components that coordinate through learned connections.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Core Principle</strong>: Intelligence emerges from connections, not from size.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">The Problem: Black Box AI</h2><a id="user-content-the-problem-black-box-ai" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: The Problem: Black Box AI" href="#the-problem-black-box-ai"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Traditional language models are opaque:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>You can't see <em>why</em> they made a decision</li>
<li>You can't trace the reasoning path</li>
<li>You can't debug or fix specific behaviors</li>
<li>You can't inspect what they learned</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">The POLLN Solution</h2><a id="user-content-the-polln-solution" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: The POLLN Solution" href="#the-polln-solution"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">POLLN replaces one giant model with <strong>hundreds of tiny agents</strong>, each doing one thing well. Every agent-to-agent communication is visible, traceable, and debuggable.</p>

</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Key Innovation: A2A Packages</h3><a id="user-content-key-innovation-a2a-packages" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Key Innovation: A2A Packages" href="#key-innovation-a2a-packages"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Every communication between agents is an <strong>A2A (Agent-to-Agent) Package</strong>—a JSON artifact that contains:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>The decision or data being passed</li>
<li>The reasoning trace</li>
<li>Lineage (what inputs led here)</li>
<li>Causal chain ID (for replay and debugging)</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">This makes every agent decision:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Inspectable</strong> - See exactly what happened</li>
<li><strong>Replayable</strong> - Re-run any decision</li>
<li><strong>Debuggable</strong> - Find and fix flaws</li>
<li><strong>Replaceable</strong> - Swap one agent without affecting others</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Core Concepts</h2><a id="user-content-core-concepts" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Core Concepts" href="#core-concepts"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">LOG (Ledger-Organizing Graph)</h3><a id="user-content-log-ledger-organizing-graph" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: LOG (Ledger-Organizing Graph)" href="#log-ledger-organizing-graph"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">A <strong>LOG</strong> is a graph structure where:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Ledger</strong>: Every decision is recorded and traceable</li>
<li><strong>Organizing</strong>: Information structures itself through use</li>
<li><strong>Graph</strong>: Intelligence emerges from connections</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Memory in POLLN is <strong>structural</strong>, not representational. The system doesn't store facts—it stores stronger connections between agents that work well together.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Quick Analogy: POLLN and Bees</h3><a id="user-content-quick-analogy-polln-and-bees" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Quick Analogy: POLLN and Bees" href="#quick-analogy-polln-and-bees"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">SMPbots: Tile Intelligence for Spreadsheets</h2><a id="user-content-smpbots-tile-intelligence-for-spreadsheets" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: SMPbots: Tile Intelligence for Spreadsheets" href="#smpbots-tile-intelligence-for-spreadsheets"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>SMP = Seed + Model + Prompt</strong></p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Tile Intelligence in real-time spreadsheets for simulation or monitoring. Deconstruct Agents into Essential functions for granular reasoning control and reverse engineering logic visually. SMPbots Seed+Model+Prompt can replace blurry logic if cell is functioning optimum and can scale. Inductive ML Programming in Spreadsheets or Embedded Headless.</strong></p>
<p dir="auto">POLLN introduces <strong>SMPbots</strong>—a programming approach that's as stable as a CPU script but scales naturally on GPU hardware. Load the model once, run thousands of calculations in parallel on CUDA or Kubernetes, locally or in the cloud.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>GPU-Stable Programming for CUDA and Kubernetes</strong></p>
<p dir="auto">At the surface level, SMP is approachable for spreadsheet users. Under the hood, it's a GPU-native programming model that scales horizontally across CUDA cores and Kubernetes clusters without code changes.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Tile Intelligence: Deconstructing Agents into Essential Functions</h3><a id="user-content-tile-intelligence-deconstructing-agents-into-essential-functions" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Tile Intelligence: Deconstructing Agents into Essential Functions" href="#tile-intelligence-deconstructing-agents-into-essential-functions"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Traditional AI agents are monolithic black boxes. POLLN <strong>deconstructs agents into tiles</strong>—essential functions that provide:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Granular reasoning control</strong> - See each step of the logic</li>
<li><strong>Visual reverse engineering</strong> - Understand how decisions emerge</li>
<li><strong>Composable intelligence</strong> - Mix and match tiles like LEGO</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Why "SMP" Sounds Simple But Isn't</h3><a id="user-content-why-smp-sounds-simple-but-isnt" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Why "SMP" Sounds Simple But Isn't" href="#why-smp-sounds-simple-but-isnt"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The name is catchy because it sounds like "simple person programming"—and it <em>is</em> simple for the user. But that simplicity doesn't mean it's not powerful. In fact, for spreadsheet applications, SMP Programming is <strong>often better</strong> than complex AI systems.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Inductive ML Programming in Spreadsheets:</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Learn patterns FROM data, not just apply rules TO data</li>
<li>Each cell induces its own logic from examples</li>
<li>Embedded headless operation—no UI needed, just calculation</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Why SMPbots Are Perfect for Spreadsheets</h3><a id="user-content-why-smpbots-are-perfect-for-spreadsheets" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Why SMPbots Are Perfect for Spreadsheets" href="#why-smpbots-are-perfect-for-spreadsheets"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Traditional approach:</strong> Call an external API for each cell calculation</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>❌ Network latency (100-500ms per call)</li>
<li>❌ Rate limits and costs</li>
<li>❌ No offline capability</li>
<li>❌ Can't parallelize efficiently</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><strong>SMPbots approach:</strong> Run calculations in parallel with in-memory models</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>✅ Millisecond response times (model already loaded)</li>
<li>✅ Thousands of parallel calculations on CUDA cores</li>
<li>✅ Scales horizontally on Kubernetes clusters</li>
<li>✅ Same code runs locally or in the cloud</li>
<li>✅ Works offline after initial load</li>
<li>✅ Perfect for batch operations</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><strong>GPU Programming, Simplified:</strong></p>
<p dir="auto">SMP scripts are as stable as traditional CPU code, but they execute on GPU hardware when available. The runtime handles:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>CUDA memory management automatically</li>
<li>Kubernetes pod scheduling for distributed workloads</li>
<li>Fallback to CPU when GPU unavailable</li>
<li>No code changes needed for different deployment targets</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">The "Simple Person Programming" Advantage</h3><a id="user-content-the-simple-person-programming-advantage" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: The "Simple Person Programming" Advantage" href="#the-simple-person-programming-advantage"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">SMP Programming democratizes AI development:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>Traditional AI Engineer:
  "I need to fine-tune a transformer, set up inference,
   deploy a microservice, handle authentication..."

SMP Programmer (Spreadsheet User):
  "I just want this cell to tell me if sales are trending up."
  =SMP("trend", A1:A100, "up or down?")
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">The complexity is <strong>handled by the system</strong>, not the user. The user gets powerful AI without needing to be an AI engineer.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">The Parallel Processing Advantage</h3><a id="user-content-the-parallel-processing-advantage" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: The Parallel Processing Advantage" href="#the-parallel-processing-advantage"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">When you have thousands of cells running SMPbots in parallel:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>Traditional sequential processing:
Cell 1 → Cell 2 → Cell 3 → ... → Cell 1000
(500ms × 1000 = 8.3 minutes)

SMP parallel processing:
All 1000 cells → simultaneously
(500ms total, same hardware)
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="Traditional sequential processing:
Cell 1 → Cell 2 → Cell 3 → ... → Cell 1000
(500ms × 1000 = 8.3 minutes)

SMP parallel processing:
All 1000 cells → simultaneously
(500ms total, same hardware)" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto"><strong>That's 1000x faster</strong> for spreadsheet operations.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Why "Simple" Doesn't Mean "Dumb"</h3><a id="user-content-why-simple-doesnt-mean-dumb" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Why "Simple" Doesn't Mean "Dumb"" href="#why-simple-doesnt-mean-dumb"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">SMP Programming may be simple to use, but under the hood it leverages:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>KV-Cache System</strong> - Shared context between cells</li>
<li><strong>Hebbian Learning</strong> - Cells that work well together wire together</li>
<li><strong>Plinko Selection</strong> - Probabilistic choice for diversity</li>
<li><strong>World Model</strong> - Internal representation for dreaming</li>
<li><strong>Federated Learning</strong> - Knowledge sharing across cells</li>
<li><strong>CUDA-K8s Runtime</strong> - Automatic GPU scaling and cluster orchestration</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">The simplicity is in the <strong>interface</strong>, not the <strong>implementation</strong>.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>GPU Programming That Just Works:</strong></p>
<p dir="auto">SMP scripts are portable across execution environments:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Write once on your laptop (CPU or GPU)</li>
<li>Deploy to Kubernetes cluster (distributed GPU)</li>
<li>Scale horizontally without code changes</li>
<li>Runtime handles the complexity</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">SMPbot Lifecycle</h3><a id="user-content-smpbot-lifecycle" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: SMPbot Lifecycle" href="#smpbot-lifecycle"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              SMPBOT LIFECYCLE                               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│   1. SEED    User provides data (A1:A100)                   │
│      │                                                       │
│      ▼                                                       │
│   2. MODEL   System loads appropriate model                   │
│      │       (trend-analyzer, cached in memory)             │
│      ▼                                                       │
│   3. PROMPT  Task is configured                             │
│      │       ("analyze upward trend")                       │
│      ▼                                                       │
│   4. EXECUTE Model processes seed with prompt               │
│      │       (parallel with other cells)                    │
│      ▼                                                       │
│   5. LEARN  Feedback updates model (Hebbian learning)        │
│      │                                                       │
│      ▼                                                       │
│   6. REMEMBER  Next time is faster (KV-Cache)               │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">Learning = strengthening connections between agents that work well together.</p>

</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">POLLN maintains multiple variants of each capability:</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">5. Plinko Selection (Probabilistic Choice)</h3><a id="user-content-5-plinko-selection-probabilistic-choice" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 5. Plinko Selection (Probabilistic Choice)" href="#5-plinko-selection-probabilistic-choice"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">POLLN doesn't pick the "best" option. It samples probabilistically:</p>
<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto"><pre class="notranslate"><code>         │
    ┌────┴────┐
    │ PLINKO  │
    │ LAYER   │
    └─────────┘
      ╱   ╲   ╱
    Option Option Option
      A      B      C

    P(A) = 0.6   ← Usually best
    P(B) = 0.3   ← Sometimes good
    P(C) = 0.1   ← Explores rarely
</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="         │
    ┌────┴────┐
    │ PLINKO  │
    │ LAYER   │
    └─────────┘
      ╱   ╲   ╱
    Option Option Option
      A      B      C

    P(A) = 0.6   ← Usually best
    P(B) = 0.3   ← Sometimes good
    P(C) = 0.1   ← Explores rarely" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto"><strong>Why?</strong> Exploration keeps the system from getting stuck. Temperature controls exploration vs exploitation.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">The Spreadsheet Tool: LOG Integration</h2><a id="user-content-the-spreadsheet-tool-log-integration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: The Spreadsheet Tool: LOG Integration" href="#the-spreadsheet-tool-log-integration"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">POLLN's killer app is a <strong>spreadsheet integration</strong> where every cell can contain an agent.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>What users see</strong>:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Type <code>=AGENT("Analyze Q3 sales", A1:A100)</code> in a cell</li>
<li>Agents emerge, learn, and optimize</li>
<li>Double-click any cell to inspect the reasoning</li>
<li>Simulate "what if" scenarios without affecting production</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto"><strong>What makes it different</strong>:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Inspectable</strong>: See exactly how each decision was made</li>
<li><strong>Open Source</strong>: Free forever, MIT license</li>
<li><strong>Learns</strong>: Gets smarter with use</li>
<li><strong>Understandable</strong>: No black boxes</li>
</ul>
<hr>

<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Term</th>
<th>Definition</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Agent</strong></td>
<td>A specialized component that performs one task well</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Colony</strong></td>
<td>A collection of agents working together</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>A2A Package</strong></td>
<td>Agent-to-Agent communication artifact (fully traceable)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Plinko Layer</strong></td>
<td>Stochastic selection mechanism (probabilistic choice)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Hebbian Learning</strong></td>
<td>"Neurons that fire together, wire together"</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Subsumption</strong></td>
<td>Layered processing where lower layers override higher ones</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>LOG</strong></td>
<td>Ledger-Organizing Graph (or Logic Graph)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>KV-Cache</strong></td>
<td>Efficient context sharing between agents</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Distillation</strong></td>
<td>Large model teaching small agents</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>World Model</strong></td>
<td>Internal representation for planning/dreaming</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>

<ul dir="auto">

  <h2 class="tmp-mb-3 h4">About</h2>

      <p class="f4 tmp-my-3">
        SuoerInstance LOG Visualized in Spreadsheets for Tile Intelligence in real-time workflows, simulations or monitoring. Deconstruct Agents into Essential functions for granulate reasoning control and reverse engineering logic visually. SMPbots Seed+Model+Prompt can replace cold logic and scale on GPUs. Inductive ML Programs Instances in cell logic.
      </p>
